testcase.name: test_main_download_video_with_quality

failure.message: assert 1 == 0 + where 1 = <Result AttributeError("'Downloader' object has no attribute 'qualities'")>.exit_code

testcase.failure:
def test_main_download_video_with_quality(): runner = CliRunner() with patch( "apyrat.apyrat.Downloader._get_available_qualities", return_value=["480", "720", "1080"], ) as mock_qualities: result = runner.invoke( main, [ "https://www.aparat.com/v/qur3I", "-q", "720", ], ) > assert result.exit_code == 0 E assert 1 == 0 E + where 1 = <Result AttributeError("'Downloader' object has no attribute 'qualities'")>.exit_code
tests/cli_test.py:21: AssertionError

testcase.name: test_main_download_video_without_quality

failure.message: assert 1 == 0 + where 1 = <Result AttributeError("'Downloader' object has no attribute 'qualities'")>.exit_code

testcase.failure:
def test_main_download_video_without_quality(): runner = CliRunner() with patch( "apyrat.apyrat.Downloader._get_available_qualities", return_value=["480", "720", "1080"], ), patch("click.prompt", return_value="720"): result = runner.invoke(main, ["https://www.aparat.com/v/qur3I"]) > assert result.exit_code == 0 E assert 1 == 0 E + where 1 = <Result AttributeError("'Downloader' object has no attribute 'qualities'")>.exit_code
tests/cli_test.py:32: AssertionError

testcase.name: test_get_quality_not_available
testcase.failure:
def test_get_quality_not_available(): downloader = Downloader("https://www.aparat.com/v/qur3I") downloader.qualities = ["480", "720", "1080"] with patch("click.prompt", return_value="720"): > assert get_quality(downloader, "240") == "720" ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
tests/cli_test.py:46: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ apyrat/cli.py:92: in get_quality type=click.Choice(downloader._get_available_qualities()), ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
self = <apyrat.apyrat.Downloader object at 0x10646bce0>
def _get_available_qualities(self): qualities = set( video_quality.get("profile") > for video in self.videos ^^^^^^^^^^^ for video_quality in video ) E TypeError: 'coroutine' object is not iterable
apyrat/apyrat.py:107: TypeError

testcase.name: test_get_quality_not_available_no_confirm
testcase.failure:
def test_get_quality_not_available_no_confirm(): downloader = Downloader("https://www.aparat.com/v/qur3I") downloader.qualities = ["480", "720", "1080"] with patch("click.prompt", return_value="480"): > assert get_quality(downloader, "240") == "480" ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
tests/cli_test.py:53: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ apyrat/cli.py:92: in get_quality type=click.Choice(downloader._get_available_qualities()), ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
self = <apyrat.apyrat.Downloader object at 0x1065d4770>
def _get_available_qualities(self): qualities = set( video_quality.get("profile") > for video in self.videos ^^^^^^^^^^^ for video_quality in video ) E TypeError: 'coroutine' object is not iterable
apyrat/apyrat.py:107: TypeError

testcase.name: test_get_available_videos_single_video

failure.message: TypeError: object of type 'coroutine' has no len()

testcase.name: test_download_when_file_exists

failure.message: AssertionError: Expected 'download' to have been called once. Called 0 times.

testcase.failure:
mock_wget = <MagicMock name='download' id='4404448288'> mock_isfile = <MagicMock name='isfile' id='4404448624'>
@patch("os.path.isfile", return_value=True) @patch("wget.download") def test_download_when_file_exists(mock_wget, mock_isfile): downloader = Downloader("https://www.aparat.com/v/qur3I", "outputfile") downloader.videos = [ [ { "title": "My Video Title", "profile": "720p", "url": "https://link-to-video", } ] ] downloader.qualities = ["720p"] downloader.download("720p") > mock_wget.assert_called_once()
tests/test_apyrat.py:45: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
self = <MagicMock name='download' id='4404448288'>
def assert_called_once(self): """assert that the mock was called only once. """ if not self.call_count == 1: msg = ("Expected '%s' to have been called once. Called %s times.%s" % (self._mock_name or 'mock', self.call_count, self._calls_repr())) > raise AssertionError(msg) E AssertionError: Expected 'download' to have been called once. Called 0 times.
/opt/anaconda3/envs/SWA_Rep2/lib/python3.14/unittest/mock.py:964: AssertionError
